failed_cases.description: Transform renames, drops, and adds JSON-parsed default columns.
setup_sql: create table tasks(id integer primary key, name text, scratch text);
insert into tasks(id,name,scratch) values (1,'One','x'),(2,'Two','y');
errors: command 0 exit 1, expected 0; stderr="Error: Cannot set default: column 'priority' not found\n"
SQL failed 'select id, title, priority, typeof(priority), done, typeof(done) from tasks order by id': no such column: title

command_results.stderr: Error: Cannot set default: column 'priority' not found

failed_cases.description: Inserted rows are normalized, transformed, and queried without losing lookup relationships.
commands: select o.id, c.value as customer, o.amount, o.status from orders o join customers c on o.customer_id = c.id order by o.id
errors: command 2 exit 1, expected 0; stderr="Error: Cannot set default: column 'status' not found\n"
command 3 exit 1, expected 0; stderr='Error: no such column: o.amount\n'

command 3 JSON mismatch: expected [{'id': 1, 'customer': 'Ada', 'amount': 10, 'status': 'open'}, {'id': 2, 'customer': 'Lin', 'amount': 15, 'status': 'open'}, {'id': 3, 'customer': 'Ada', 'amount': 7, 'status': 'open'}], got None

command_results.stderr: Error: Cannot set default: column 'status' not found

args: select o.id, c.value as customer, o.amount, o.status from orders o join customers c on o.customer_id = c.id order by o.id
command_results.stderr: Error: no such column: o.amount

failed_cases.description: Transform preserves primary key, row count, metadata, and query behavior.
errors: command 1 exit 1, expected 0; stderr="Error: Cannot set default: column 'status' not found\n"

command 4 JSON mismatch: expected [{'id': 1, 'title': 'Hammer', 'status': 'active'}, {'id': 2, 'title': 'Nail', 'status': 'active'}], got [{'id': 1, 'name': 'Hammer', 'notes': 'old'}, {'id': 2, 'name': 'Nail', 'notes': 'tiny'}]
command 3 stdout missing 'title'; stdout='CREATE TABLE "items" ("id" INTEGER, "name" TEXT, "notes" TEXT, PRIMARY KEY ("id"))\n'
command 3 stdout missing 'status'; stdout='CREATE TABLE "items" ("id" INTEGER, "name" TEXT, "notes" TEXT, PRIMARY KEY ("id"))\n'

command_results.stderr: Error: Cannot set default: column 'status' not found

command_results.stdout: CREATE TABLE "items" ("id" INTEGER, "name" TEXT, "notes" TEXT, PRIMARY KEY ("id"))

failed_cases.description: CSV inferred types, omitted upsert values, and JSON-parsed transform defaults compose.

failed_cases.description: A transform rename changes the column name that a later extract must normalize.
commands: select b.id, b.title, c.value as category, b.status from books b join categories c on b.category_id = c.id order by b.id
errors: command 1 exit 1, expected 0; stderr="Error: Cannot set default: column 'status' not found\n"
command 2 exit 1, expected 0; stderr="Error: Column 'category' not found in table 'books'\n"
command 3 exit 1, expected 0; stderr='Error: no such table: categories\n'

command 3 JSON mismatch: expected [{'id': 1, 'title': 'A', 'category': 'sci-fi', 'status': 'active'}, {'id': 2, 'title': 'B', 'category': 'memoir', 'status': 'active'}, {'id': 3, 'title': 'C', 'category': 'sci-fi', 'status': 'active'}], got None
SQL failed 'select id, value from categories order by id': no such table: categories

command_results.stderr: Error: Cannot set default: column 'status' not found
Error: Column 'category' not found in table 'books'

args: select b.id, b.title, c.value as category, b.status from books b join categories c on b.category_id = c.id order by b.id
command_results.stderr: Error: no such table: categories

failed_cases.description: An extract-created lookup id column can be renamed by a later transform while preserving the lookup relationship.
commands: select i.id, i.summary, s.value as state, i.triaged from issues i join states s on i.workflow_state_id = s.id order by i.id
errors: command 2 exit 1, expected 0; stderr="Error: Cannot set default: column 'triaged' not found\n"
command 3 exit 1, expected 0; stderr='Error: no such column: i.triaged\n'

command 3 JSON mismatch: expected [{'id': 1, 'summary': 'One', 'state': 'open', 'triaged': 0}, {'id': 2, 'summary': 'Two', 'state': 'closed', 'triaged': 0}, {'id': 3, 'summary': 'Three', 'state': 'open', 'triaged': 0}], got None

args: select i.id, i.summary, s.value as state, i.triaged from issues i join states s on i.workflow_state_id = s.id order by i.id
command_results.stderr: Error: no such column: i.triaged

all_cases.description: JSON insert creates a typed table with a single-column primary key.
CSV insert infers integer, real, boolean, and text columns.
Upsert updates matching primary-key rows while preserving omitted old values.
setup_sql: create table dogs(id integer primary key, name text, age integer, breed text);
insert into dogs(id,name,age,breed) values (1,'Cleo',4,'tabby'),(2,'Nixie',2,null);

all_cases.description: Compound-key upsert with alter adds new columns and preserves omitted values.
setup_sql: create table scores(team text, week integer, score integer, note text, primary key(team, week));
insert into scores(team,week,score,note) values ('red',1,10,'keep'),('red',2,11,'old'),('blue',1,7,'blue');

all_cases.description: Rows command applies where, order, and limit to an existing table.
setup_sql: create table pets(id integer primary key, name text, age integer);
insert into pets(id,name,age) values (1,'Cleo',4),(2,'Pancakes',2),(3,'Miso',7);

all_cases.description: Query command returns JSON row objects for arbitrary SQL.
setup_sql: create table pets(id integer primary key, name text, age integer);
insert into pets(id,name,age) values (1,'Cleo',4),(2,'Pancakes',2),(3,'Miso',7);
commands: select name, age from pets where age > 3 order by name

args: select name, age from pets where age > 3 order by name

all_cases.description: Tables with counts and schema reflect existing database metadata.
setup_sql: create table pets(id integer primary key, name text);
insert into pets(id,name) values (1,'Cleo'),(2,'Miso');

command_results.stdout: CREATE TABLE pets(id integer primary key, name text)

all_cases.description: Extract normalizes repeated text values into a lookup table.
setup_sql: create table orders(id integer primary key, customer text, total integer);
insert into orders(id,customer,total) values (1,'Ada',10),(2,'Lin',15),(3,'Ada',7);

all_cases.description: Extract ignores null values and assigns lookup IDs in first-seen order.
setup_sql: create table events(id integer primary key, kind text);
insert into events(id,kind) values (1,'beta'),(2,null),(3,'alpha'),(4,'beta');

all_cases.description: Enable-FTS builds an FTS table for an existing document table.
setup_sql: create table docs(id integer primary key, title text, body text);
insert into docs(id,title,body) values (1,'Alpha','red apple harvest'),(2,'Beta','green pear orchard');

command_results.stdout: FTS enabled on 'docs' for columns: title, body

all_cases.description: Search returns original rows from an existing FTS index.
setup_sql: create table docs(id integer primary key, title text, body text);
insert into docs(id,title,body) values (1,'Alpha','red apple harvest'),(2,'Beta','green pear orchard');
create virtual table docs_fts using fts5(title, body, content='docs', content_rowid='id');
insert into docs_fts(rowid,title,body) values (1,'Alpha','red apple harvest'),(2,'Beta','green pear orchard');
